headline: Tesla Launches Full Self Driving In Europe, Stock Falls
summary: Geopolitics trumped autonomous driving news early Monday, leaving  Tesla  stock mired in its continuing slump.  The moves came after President Donald Trump said the U.S. Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks with Iran failed to yield a satisfactory deal over the weekend.  The dip for Tesla stock also came after the Netherlands approved Tesla’s Full Self-Driving driver-assistance feature.

content.title: Tesla Launches Full Self Driving In Europe, Stock Falls

The moves came after President Donald Trump said the U.S. Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks with Iran failed to yield a satisfactory deal over the weekend.  The dip for Tesla stock also came after the Netherlands approved Tesla’s Full Self-Driving driver-assistance feature.

headline: Dutch regulator to seek EU approval for Tesla's self-driving software
summary: Dutch vehicle authority RDW has notified the European Commission of its plan to seek European Union-wide ‌approval for Tesla's Full Self-Driving software system, the regulator's general manager of type ‌approvals told Reuters on Monday.  Bernd van Nieuwenhoven emphasised that the FSD system, despite its name, is driver ​assistance software rather than a full self-driving car.  Van Nieuwenhoven said he was confident it could be used safely on all roads in the Netherlands, including ‌Amsterdam, where electric cars share ⁠narrow streets with motorized vehicles, bikes and e-bikes.

content.title: Dutch regulator to seek EU approval for Tesla's self-driving software
content.summary: Dutch vehicle authority RDW has notified the European Commission of its plan to seek European Union-wide ‌approval for Tesla's Full Self-Driving software system, the regulator's general manager of type ‌approvals told Reuters on Monday.  Bernd van Nieuwenhoven emphasised that the FSD system, despite its name, is driver ​assistance software rather than a full self-driving car.  Van Nieuwenhoven said he was confident it could be used safely on all roads in the Netherlands, including ‌Amsterdam, where electric cars share ⁠narrow streets with motorized vehicles, bikes and e-bikes.

headline: Batteries Would Take Tesla To All-Time High
summary: Tesla’s (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock hit an all-time high in October 2025. It has collapsed since then. Year to date, it is down 22% against a flat market. headline: Tesla Launches Full Self Driving in Europe. Why the Stock Is Still in a Slump.
summary: Geopolitics dented autonomous driving news early Monday, leaving  Tesla  stock struggling to get out of its slump.  The moves came after President Donald Trump said the U.S. Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks with Iran failed to yield a satisfactory deal over the weekend.  The muted move for Tesla stock also came after the Netherlands approved Tesla’s Full Self-Driving driver-assistance feature.

content.title: Tesla Launches Full Self Driving in Europe. Why the Stock Is Still in a Slump.

The moves came after President Donald Trump said the U.S. Navy would blockade the Strait of Hormuz after peace talks with Iran failed to yield a satisfactory deal over the weekend.  The muted move for Tesla stock also came after the Netherlands approved Tesla’s Full Self-Driving driver-assistance feature.

headline: Tesla FSD Win In Europe And Compact SUV Revival Reshape Growth Story
summary: Dutch regulators have approved Tesla’s supervised Full Self Driving (FSD) system, the first such green light in Europe. The approval opens the door for supervised FSD use on Dutch roads and could influence how other European regulators approach similar software. Tesla, NasdaqGS:TSLA, is also reviving plans for a lower cost compact SUV aimed at China and global markets after previously shelving the project. The compact SUV initiative points to a shift in Tesla’s product roadmap as it responds...
